content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — privacy policy claims "We do not collect, process, or store your location data" but App/RunSessionManager.swift:1-16 imports CoreLocation, requests when-in-use authorization, and stores GPS locations in `route`; description.txt also advertises GPS run mapping.
Pierre: Apple cross-checks the privacy policy against actual data practices, and a direct contradiction like this is a classic 5.1.1 rejection trigger. The app plainly collects and stores location (route history, potentially synced to iCloud), yet the policy denies it outright. Update the policy to accurately describe location collection, on-device/iCloud storage, and retention before submission.
